title: A zero-scale canonical KdV update leaves the state unchanged

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the scale is zero, the canonical KdV conservative update is the identity update.
assumptions: all scalar parameters are real numbers
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n and i are natural-number grid or range indices as used in the theorem statement
the KdV scaffold definitions are those in LeanNumerics.FiniteDifference.Kdv
informal.expected_conclusion: The zero-scale canonical update returns the original state.

declarations.name: kdvCanonicalUpdate_zero_scale
declarations.statement: theorem kdvCanonicalUpdate_zero_scale (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : kdvCanonicalUpdate 0 u i = u i

tasks.input: Translate this KdV conservative scaffold theorem into Lean 4: If the scale is zero, the canonical KdV conservative update is the identity update.
tasks.target: theorem kdvCanonicalUpdate_zero_scale (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : kdvCanonicalUpdate 0 u i = u i

tasks.input: Given the KdV scaffold definitions and referenced conservation lemmas, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding kdvCanonicalUpdate and reusing kdvConservativeUpdate_zero_scale.
